Day 2 — PWA First Look: Core Features
Last post had a lot of talk and some examples. So what makes PWA
? You probably already got a feel for it, but let me lay out the main features.
Reliable means your app can still load even when the user's network is bad. This relies almost entirely on Service Workers (more on those later).

You know how some mobile apps still load even on bad connections or in airplane mode? PWA gives you the same superpower — offline caching. On bad or no network, your cached assets keep the web app working.
Otherwise you'd be looking at the offline dinosaur
That's one of PWA's biggest selling points.
Smooth screen transitions and a proper splash screen on launch.
Engaging is about making the app feel as close to native as possible.
It breaks down into three things:
PWA is installable as an app — just add to home screen via the browser, no need to download from an app store.
PWA feels like using a native app.
The unique part: PWA supports push notifications even when offline, which helps bring users back.
For the rest, I'll quote a detailed list from a Taiwanese iThome ironman series:

- Progressive
  - Works for every user with basic browsing, and uses stronger features when supported.
- Responsive
  - Responsive UI, optimised across different devices.
- Connectivity independent
  - Doesn't depend on the network — Service Workers let users browse on low bandwidth or even offline.
- App-like
  - Feels like an app, with app-like loading speed for a better user experience.
- Fresh
  - Automatically updates content via Service Workers.
- Safe
  - Served over HTTPS to keep data secure.
- Discoverable
  - SEO-optimisable, so users can find the site easily and you get more reach.
- Re-engageable
  - Push notifications and similar features let you re-engage users and lift return rates.
- Installable
  - "Add to Home" gives you an app-like icon on the home screen, no App Store install required.
- Linkable
  - Shareable via URL.
- Works for every user with basic browsing, and uses stronger features when supported.
- Responsive UI, optimised across different devices.
- Doesn't depend on the network — Service Workers let users browse on low bandwidth or even offline.
- Feels like an app, with app-like loading speed for a better user experience.
- Automatically updates content via Service Workers.
- Served over HTTPS to keep data secure.
- SEO-optimisable, so users can find the site easily and you get more reach.
- Push notifications and similar features let you re-engage users and lift return rates.
- "Add to Home" gives you an app-like icon on the home screen, no App Store install required.
